Trends & Insights

Nature-Inspired Baby Names: The Rise of Earthy Choices

Explore the growing trend of nature-inspired baby names, from classic flower names to modern wilderness picks, with popularity data.

By Ziven Borceg7 min readJanuary 5, 2026

Nature names have blossomed from a niche hippie trend into one of the most significant movements in modern baby naming. From classic Rose to adventurous Bear, parents are finding inspiration in the natural world.

Categories of Nature Names

Botanical Names

The largest category, including flowers, trees, and plants:

  • Flowers: Rose, Lily, Violet, Iris, Dahlia, Jasmine, Poppy
  • Trees: Willow, Rowan, Aspen, Cedar, Linden, Hazel
  • Plants: Ivy, Fern, Sage, Clover, Briar, Juniper

    • Animal Names

  • Birds: Wren, Robin, Lark, Phoebe, Starling
  • Wild: Fox, Bear, Wolf, Hawk, Falcon
  • Gentle: Fawn, Dove, Bunny

    • Celestial Names

  • Stars: Stella, Nova, Vega, Sirius
  • Moon: Luna, Selene, Artemis
  • Sky: Aurora, Dawn, Twilight, Celeste

    • Earth & Water

  • Earth: Clay, Stone, Flint, Terra
  • Water: River, Brook, Lake, Ocean, Marina, Coral
  • Weather: Storm, Rain, Tempest, Misty

    • The Popularity Data

    Nature name usage has increased 350% since 2000:

  • 2000: ~2% of all baby names had nature origins
  • 2010: ~4%
  • 2020: ~7%
  • 2025: ~9%

    • The fastest risers include Willow (+2,400%), Ivy (+1,800%), and River (+3,200%) over the past two decades.

    Why Nature Names Resonate

    In an increasingly digital world, nature names connect children to the organic world. They also tend to age well, work internationally, and carry inherently positive associations.
